Functions to initialize services required by the kernel.

# Purpose
This C header file declares two functions, [`services_early_init`](<#services_early_init>) and [`services_init`](<#services_init>), which are used to initialize services that the kernel depends on. The [`services_early_init`](<#services_early_init>) function is likely intended for early-stage initialization tasks, while [`services_init`](<#services_init>) is for subsequent initialization processes. The `#pragma once` directive ensures that the file is included only once in a single compilation, preventing duplicate definitions. The file includes a copyright notice and licensing information under the Apache License, Version 2.0.
# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### services\_early\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:services_early_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/services/services.h#L20>)

Initialize services required by the kernel.
- **Description**: Use this function to initialize essential services that the kernel needs before it can operate. This function must be called during the early stages of system startup, before any other service initialization functions. It is important to ensure that this function is not called in recovery firmware mode, as it is conditionally compiled to exclude certain initializations in that context.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`services_early_init`](<services.c.md#services_early_init>)  (Implementation)


---
### services\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:services_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/services/services.h#L22>)

Initializes services required by the kernel.
- **Description**: Use this function to initialize the necessary services that the kernel depends on. It must be called during the system startup sequence to ensure that all required services are available for the kernel to function correctly. This function handles both common and normal service initialization, except when the `RECOVERY_FW` flag is defined, in which case only common services are initialized. Ensure that this function is called after `services_early_init` if it is part of the initialization sequence.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`services_init`](<services.c.md#services_init>)  (Implementation)
